title: Picky Eating Meltdowns: The 3-Plate Method That Actually Works
excerpt: End mealtime arguments with a simple plate format that keeps nutrition and peace on the same table.
coverAlt: Colorful toddler meal plate on a table

content.text: Fix Mealtime Stress Without Forcing Bites
When every dinner ends in pleading or tears, it can feel personal. It is usually sensory overload plus pressure, not stubbornness, and that means the solution is structure, not force.

content.caption: Safety foods + exposure foods lower resistance.

content.text: The 3-plate method lowers fear by guaranteeing one accepted option while still exposing children to new food. Repeated low-pressure exposure builds acceptance over time.
Parents own the schedule and menu. Children own intake. This split dramatically cuts power struggles and protects appetite regulation.

items: Plate 3: tiny taste of a new food.
Track table time, not just bites.

content.text: Nutrition improves faster when meals feel safe. Calm consistency is more powerful than one dramatic dinner victory.
